reasoning_content: We need to check if 193 is prime. Quick sanity: check divisibility by small primes. 193 is odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits 1+9+3=13, not divisible by 3. Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*27=189, remainder 4, not divisible. 11: 11*17=187, remainder 6; 11*18=198 too high. 13: 13*14=182, remainder 11; 13*15=195 too high. 17: 17*11=187, remainder 6; 17*12=204 too high. 19: 19*10=190, remainder 3; 19*11=209 too high. Next prime 23: 23*8=184, remainder 9; 23*9=207 too high. 29: 29*6=174, remainder 19; 29*7=203 too high. 31: 31*6=186, remainder 7; 31*7=217 too high. 37: 37*5=185, remainder 8; 37*6=222 too high. 41: 41*4=164, remainder 29; 41*5=205 too high. 43: 43*4=172, remainder 21; 43*5=215 too high. 47: 47*4=188, remainder 5; 47*5=235. 53: 53*3=159, remainder 34; 53*4=212 too high. 59: 59*3=177, remainder 16; 59*4=236. 61: 61*3=183, remainder 10; 61*4=244. 67: 67*2=134, remainder 59; 67*3=201 too high. 71: 71*2=142, remainder 51; 71*3=213. 73: 73*2=146, remainder 47; 73*3=219. 79: 79*2=158, remainder 35; 79*3=237. 83: 83*2=166, remainder 27; 83*3=249. 89: 89*2=178, remainder 15; 89*3=267. 97: 97*2=194, remainder -1, so 97*2=194 >193, so 97*1=97, remainder 96. So no divisors found. Square root of 193 is about 13.89, so we only need to check primes up to 13. Actually, sqrt(193) ≈ 13.89, so we only need to check primes ≤13: 2,3,5,7,11,13. We already checked all those. 13 gave remainder 11. So 193 is prime. Quick sanity: yes, 193 is prime.
